section.title: Part IV: The Architectural Blueprint - A Three-Pillar Integrated Model
section.para: The proposed solution is an integrated ecosystem of three distinct legal entities working in synergy.

listitem.para: Function: Serves as the community's internal, cashless marketplace. It facilitates the exchange of goods and services between members using a non-monetary internal accounting system called "Patronage Credits," creating a vibrant barter economy that is invisible to benefits agencies.

section.title: Pillar 2: The Pooled Trust (The Financial Firewall)

listitem.para: Legal Structure: Non-Profit organization managing a Pooled Special Needs Trust.

Function: Acts as the qualified intermediary and financial firewall. When a member earns money from external work, the net pay is deposited directly into their personal sub-account within the Trust. The Trustee then uses these funds, at the direction of the member, to make benefit-compliant vendor payments. This process prevents the member from ever having direct receipt of countable income.

section.title: Pillar 3: The Social Enterprise Worker Cooperative (The Engine of Opportunity)

listitem.para: Legal Structure: A Social Purpose or Benefit Corporation with a worker cooperative governance model.

Function: Serves as the formal employer and the bridge to the external, cash-based economy. It secures external contracts and hires members as formal W-2 employees, providing them with legitimate employment to satisfy any work requirements. It handles all payroll and administrative complexities.
